reasoning: May 2025 GISTEMP anomaly was 1.07. September 2025 ERA5 anomaly was 0.66 (above 1991-2020), which usually translates to approximately 1.16 in GISTEMP (since GISTEMP is baseline 1951-1980, adding ~0.50 to ERA5). If October 2025 is similar to September 2025 but perhaps slightly cooler or relatively stable, its anomaly is very likely to fall in the 1.05 to 1.20 range. The 1.10-1.14 bracket is directly in the center of this projected range. Assuming a normal distribution around 1.14 with a standard deviation of about 0.06 (typical month-to-month variation + forecast uncertainty), the probability of landing in this specific 0.05-wide bracket is roughly 15-25%.
